title: Jenkins の 403 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: Most factual claims are well-supported by official Jenkins documentation or reliable community resources.
The article accurately identifies common causes and provides correct resolution steps.
Some claims about 'most common' scenarios are harder to definitively source but are plausible best practices.
A few sources are older (2017-2018), but the core Jenkins security and API concepts they support remain relevant and are often corroborated by newer sources or official documentation.

sources.title: Remote Access API - Jenkins
sources.claim: The official Jenkins Remote API documentation describes the detailed specifications for API calls.

sources.title: Role-based Access Control Plugin
sources.claim: If you want to check permission settings, refer to the Role-Based Access Control plugin documentation.

sources.title: CSRF Protection - Jenkins
sources.claim: Jenkins : CSRF Protection. Created by Unknown User (kohsuke), last modified by Unknown User (wilfredh) on Feb 19, 2018. Requests sent using the POST method are subject to CSRF protection in Jenkins and generally need to provide a crumb. This also applies to scripted clients that authenticate using username and password. The documentation on this page applies to Jenkins 2.222 or newer. To protect against this class of attacks, go to "Manage Jenkins" > "Configure Global Security" and select "Prevent Cross Site Request Forgery exploits." This option is enabled by default in new installations starting Jenkins 2.x. Alternatively authenticate using username and API token. Requests authenticating with an API token are exempt from CSRF protection in Jenkins. To disable CSRF protection, set the system property hudson.security.csrf.GlobalCrumbIssuerConfiguration.DISABLE_CSRF_PROTECTION to true on startup.

sources.title: How to Get the API Token for Jenkins | Baeldung on Ops
sources.claim: How to get an API token: Log in to the Jenkins instance as an administrator; Click on “Manage Jenkins” in the Jenkins dashboard; Click on the “Manage Users“; Select the user we want to generate an API token for and click on their name to access their user configuration page. Generate a token using the “Add new token” section of the user configuration page; Click on the “Copy” button to copy the token to the clipboard; Save the configurations. Jenkins provides an API token to authenticate a user and grant them access to the Jenkins API. It is often used to allow third-party applications or scripts to interact with Jenkins, such as triggering builds or accessing build artifacts.

sources.title: Jenkins CSRF CRUMB - KodeKloud Docs
sources.claim: Jenkins protects you by generating a unique, randomly created token (crumb) for each user session. This token is produced by hashing details such as the username, session ID, IP address, and a unique Jenkins salt. When a submission is made, these details must match for the crumb to be considered valid. By default, CSRF protection is enabled in Jenkins. Administrators can verify the settings by navigating to Manage Jenkins > Configure Global Security. If necessary, it is possible to disable CSRF protection by setting the following system property: hudson.security.csrf.GlobalCrumbIssuerConfiguration.DISABLE_CSRF_PROTECTION=true. To securely interact with Jenkins via the REST API, Make a POST request to the /crumbIssuer/api/json endpoint with your username and password. Upon successful authentication, Jenkins returns a JSON object with: The crumb token. The header name (typically “Jenkins-Crumb”) to be used when making subsequent requests. A Set-Cookie header that contains the session cookie.

sources.title: System Log - Jenkins
sources.claim: Jenkins uses java.util.logging for logging. The java.util.logging system by default sends every log above INFO to stdout. Jenkins is equipped with a GUI for configuring/collecting/reporting log records of your choosing. This page shows you how to do this. First, select the "System Log" from the "Manage Jenkins" page: Manage Jenkins. From there, you can create a custom log recorder, which helps you group relevant logs together while filtering out the noise.

sources.title: Script Console - Jenkins
sources.claim: Jenkins features a Groovy script console which allows one to run arbitrary Groovy scripts within the Jenkins controller runtime or in the runtime on agents. This feature can be accessed from "Manage Jenkins" > "Script Console".

sources.title: '403: No valid crumb' Jenkins GitHub webhook error fix - TheServerSide
sources.claim: A common problem DevOps professionals hit when trying to configure a GitHub webhook to remotely invoke a Jenkins build job is the dreaded 403: No valid crumb was included in the request error. This error is typically caused by using the Jenkins remote build trigger URL on the Jenkins webhook, rather than using the github-webhook URI that is configured as part of the Jenkins GitHub plugin.

sources.title: Jenkins Matrix-based security authorization - Mirantis Documentation Portal
sources.claim: The DriveTrain Jenkins uses Matrix-based security authorization by default. It allows you to grant specific permissions to users and groups. The Matrix-based security plugin enables the operator to configure the following types of permissions: Overall, Credentials, Gerrit, Agents, Job (Permissions for specific operations on Jenkins jobs, including build creation, configuration, and execution), Run, View, SCM, Metrics, Lockable resources, Artifactory. To configure the Matrix-based Security Authorization: Log in to the Jenkins web UI as an administrator. Navigate to Manage Jenkins > Configure Global Security. Scroll to the Authorization section to view and change the Matrix-based security settings.

sources.title: No Valid Crumb 403 Error Fix for Jenkins GitHub WebHooks - YouTube
sources.claim: Here's a quick fix to the Jenkins GitHub Webhook 403 error: HTTP ERROR 403 No valid crumb was included in the request.

sources.title: ログで解明、デバッグの謎 - CloudBees|テクマトリックス
sources.claim: たいていのJenkins管理者は、UIのManage Jenkins > System Logで参照できるシステムログについてはおなじみでしょう。 システムログを見ると、Jenkinsに関するjava.util.loggingの出力を参照できます。 CloudBees Jenkins Distributionでは、デフォルトでインストールされているSupport Core Pluginによって、${JENKINS_HOME}/logs/jenkins.log にもログが出力されており、中央ログサーバーにログメッセージを送信するのが容易です。

sources.title: Jenkinsのログを表示する方法 - Linux-JP.org
sources.claim: Jenkinsのログの位置は、ターゲットシステムや設定によって異なる場合があることを念頭に置いておくことは良いことです。 ただし、デフォルトでは、さまざまなシステムにおけるJenkinsログの場所は次の通りです: Linuxシステム - /var/log; Windows - %JENKINS_HOME%。JENKINS_HOMEは、ターゲットシステムにJenkinsがインストールされている場所です。 MacOS – /var/log/jenkins/.

sources.title: Jenkins Security Best Practices. 15 recommendations for hardening your… | by Erez Dasa | Medium
sources.claim: In the Matrix-based security method, we can strict the permissions for every user or group for every Jenkins option. CSRF protection uses a token (called crumb in Jenkins) created by Jenkins and sent to the user. Any form submissions or similar action resulting in modifications, like triggering builds or changing configuration, requires that the crumb be provided. The crumb contains information identifying the user it was created for, so submissions with another user's token would be rejected.

sources.title: jenkinsのJOBをAPI経由でたたいて`HTTP ERROR 403 No valid crumb was included in the request`の場合はapi tokenを発行する必要がありそう。という備忘録 - Qiita
sources.claim: JenkinsのJOBをAPI経由でたたいてHTTP ERROR 403 No valid crumb was included in the requestの場合はapi tokenを発行する必要がありそう。

sources.title: Jenkins のフリースタイルジョブを Jenkins 上の URL を叩いて実行させる方法 - 約束の地
sources.claim: CSRF対策 を有効にした場合は Crumb を使って認証する。 さらにユーザ名とパスワード（またはトークン）も一緒に送らないと認証に通らない。 叩くアドレスは http(s)://JENKINS/job/YOUR_JOB_NAME/build.

sources.title: Managing Security - Jenkins
sources.claim: Jenkins offers many configuration options for enabling, editing, or disabling various security features. The "Security" section of the web UI allows a Jenkins administrator to enable, configure, or disable key security features which apply to the entire Jenkins environment. Additionally, some plugins such as the Role-based Authorization Strategy plugin can extend the Access Control capabilities of Jenkins to support even more nuanced authentication and authorization schemes.

sources.title: Metrix-Based, Role-Based and Project-based Matrix Authentication in Jenkins | by Mahesh Parade | Medium
sources.claim: Matrix-based and Role-Based authentication is the one kind of security provided to Jenkins UI page, where to achieve this need more than one user. We can be able to give a specific permission to user to restrict the access. Role-based authorization lets you group users into roles, like admin or job creator, and manage their permissions easily.

sources.title: Securing Jenkins: Configuring CSRF Protection for Enhanced Web Application Security | by Ramkrushna Maheshwar | Medium
sources.claim: CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery) protection is a security measure implemented to prevent unauthorized or malicious actions from being executed on behalf of a user. To configure CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery) protection for Jenkins, you can follow these steps: Log in to your Jenkins instance as an administrator; Click on “Manage Jenkins” in the left-hand sidebar; In the “Configure Global Security” section, select the “Enable security” checkbox; Scroll down to the “CSRF Protection” section. After enabling CSRF protection, Jenkins will include a unique token (known as a “crumb”) in each HTTP request made from the web interface. This token is used to validate that the request originated from the Jenkins instance and not from a malicious source.

sources.title: Troubleshooting Jenkins 403 No Valid Crumb in Request Error | Baeldung on Ops
sources.claim: Jenkins remote access API is one of the powerful tools that allows automating Jenkins tasks. We can use the API to retrieve information about builds, trigger a Jenkins job, or create a new pipeline. Securing this API is crucial to protect the Jenkins instance from unwanted actions. Jenkins implements this security in multiple ways; one of them is requiring tokens to be included in the requests sent to the API.

sources.title: Matrix Authorization Strategy Plugin
sources.claim: Matrix-based security and Project-based Matrix Authorization Strategy are provided by the Matrix Authorization Strategy Plugin. This plugin defines a global matrix of permissions, allowing finer-grained authorization than built-in options.

sources.title: Managing Users - Jenkins
sources.claim: To create a user, you typically go to 'Manage Jenkins' > 'Manage Users' > 'Create User' and fill in the details. To edit user information or delete accounts, use the 'Manage Users' screen.

sources.title: Get the Jenkins API Access Token - Oracle Help Center
sources.claim: To create a token, click Add new token and immediately copy the token value. You won't be able to see the token value later and you'll need to generate another token. In the API Token section, add a new token or use the legacy token. To view the legacy token, click Show Legacy API Token and then copy the token.

sources.title: Authenticating scripted clients - Jenkins
sources.claim: When your Jenkins is secured, you can use HTTP BASIC authentication to authenticate remote API requests. API tokens are used with HTTP Basic Authentication in the format username:api-token.

unsupported_claims: 特に外部スクリプトから Jenkins API を呼ぶ場合、この原因が最も多いです。(This cause is most common, especially when calling the Jenkins API from external scripts.)

外部の CD/CD ツールや監視スクリプトから Jenkins にアクセスする際、API トークンの生成や指定を忘れると発生しやすいエラーです。(This error is likely to occur when accessing Jenkins from external CI/CD tools or monitoring scripts if the API token generation or specification is forgotten.)
Groovy スクリプト内で Jenkins API を呼ぶ場合、スクリプトの実行ユーザーの権限が適用されます。
Pipeline ステップ内で `sh` で curl を実行する場合でも、該当ユーザーに権限がなければ 403 エラーが発生します。
improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.
Optional improvement: Add more direct citations to official Jenkins documentation where applicable to strengthen the claims.

Optional improvement: For claims about commonality (e.g., 'most common cause'), consider rephrasing to emphasize common scenarios rather than making absolute statistical claims if no definitive source can be found.
Optional improvement: Verify the exact phrasing and UI paths for Jenkins versions released after the latest source dates (e.g., 2024-2025) to ensure maximum freshness.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
